findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 38. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The h1 combines 'FACT SHEET' and 'DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL' into one heading, which is acceptable, but the document header info (facility name, address, EPA ID) that appears as a centered header block in the PDF is rendered as a plain paragraph. This is a reasonable structural choice and not a significant issue.
findings.evidence:
PDF header: 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale / 6182 Old Mendenhall Road / Archdale, North Carolina / EPA ID Number: NCD 077 840 148'. HTML: h1 contains 'FACT SHEET
DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL', rest in

tag.

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> element inside the outer <main>, which is an HTML structural issue but does not cause content duplication.
findings.evidence: HTML source has <main> wrapping the whole body content, then another <main> after the <header> section.
